sections.label: Situation Overview
sections.title: What is happening right now
paragraphs: This section is generated fresh every morning by AI research. When the automation is running, today's actual news digest will appear here.
Each section covers a different angle of the conflict — from the military situation on the ground to diplomatic developments, economic impacts, and what it all means for Australia specifically.
key_facts: This is a placeholder digest — live content will appear once the automation is deployed
Sources drawn from major international wire services and verified outlets
sections.australia_relevance: Australia's position as a close US ally and significant LNG exporter means developments in the Persian Gulf directly affect Australian foreign policy and energy markets.

sections.label: Background
sections.title: How we got here — the essential context
paragraphs: Understanding current events requires context. This section always covers the historical background needed to make sense of today's news — written for readers who haven't been following closely.
It assumes no prior knowledge and avoids jargon. Think of it as the explainer you wished every news article included before diving into the day's developments.

sections.label: Diplomacy
sections.title: What the world's governments are saying and doing
paragraphs: This section tracks diplomatic activity — statements from the UN Security Council, bilateral negotiations, sanctions regimes, and the positions of key players including the US, UK, EU, Russia, China, and Australia's own government.
We flag when Australian officials make statements or when Australian policy is affected.

sections.label: Economy & Energy
sections.title: Oil, gas, and what it costs you at the bowser
paragraphs: The Strait of Hormuz carries roughly 20% of the world's oil supply. Any disruption has immediate global price consequences. This section tracks energy market movements, sanctions impacts, and what it means for Australian fuel and energy prices.
Australia is itself a major LNG exporter — which creates a complicated dual position as both potentially benefiting from higher energy prices and being exposed to supply chain disruptions.
sections.australia_relevance: Australian motorists and businesses are directly exposed to any Persian Gulf disruption through global oil price movements. The RBA monitors energy price inflation closely.

sections.label: What to watch
sections.title: The key things to follow in coming days
paragraphs: This section flags the specific developments, deadlines, and flashpoints to watch — so you know what news to pay attention to and what context to apply when it arrives.
We avoid speculation but do note where credible analysts see escalation risk or opportunity for de-escalation.
